source_intake_record.raw_question_text: As shown in the figure, in $\triangle ABC$, $\angle ACB = 90^\circ$, $AC = BC$. A straight line DE is drawn outside $\triangle ABC$ through point C, and perpendicular lines are drawn from points A and B to line DE, with the feet of the perpendiculars being E and D, respectively. If $AE=4$ and $BD=3$, then what is the length of $DE$?
source_intake_record.raw_answer_text:
\textbf{Solution:} Given that $\angle ACB=90^\circ$, \\
it follows that $\angle ACE + \angle BCD = 90^\circ$.\\
Since $\angle ACE + \angle CAE = 90^\circ$,\\
it implies that $\angle BCD = \angle CAE$.\\
In $\triangle ACE$ and $\triangle CBD$, \\
$\begin{array}{c}
\angle CAE = \angle BCD,\\
\angle E = \angle D = 90^\circ,\\
AC = BC,
\end{array}$\\
hence, $\triangle ACE \cong \triangle CBD$ by AAS,\\
thus, CE = BD = 3, CD = AE = 4,\\
therefore, DE = CE + CD = \boxed{7}.

normalization_record.normalized_question_text: In $\triangle ABC$, $\angle ACB = 90^\circ$ and $AC = BC$. A straight line $DE$ is drawn outside $\triangle ABC$ through point $C$, and perpendiculars from points $A$ and $B$ to line $DE$ meet $DE$ at $E$ and $D$, respectively. If $AE = 4$ and $BD = 3$, what is the length of $DE$?
normalization_record.normalized_answer_text: Given that $\angle ACB = 90^\circ$, we have $\angle ACE + \angle BCD = 90^\circ$. Since $\angle ACE + \angle CAE = 90^\circ$, it follows that $\angle BCD = \angle CAE$. In $\triangle ACE$ and $\triangle CBD$, $\angle CAE = \angle BCD$, $\angle E = \angle D = 90^\circ$, and $AC = BC$, so $\triangle ACE \cong \triangle CBD$ by AAS. Thus $CE = BD = 3$ and $CD = AE = 4$, therefore $DE = CE + CD = \boxed{7}$.
